notes: First-contact model + context visibility for mistral-vibe (re-assessed daemon=full after fix 4d3c2327). The adapter reads the sibling meta.json sidecar: config.active_model -> ModelName (mistral-medium-3.5, confirmed live), config.auto_compact_threshold -> ContextWindow (200000, vibe's compaction budget), and stats.context_tokens -> Tokens on turn_done. Model chip renders, token count is non-zero, and the context-utilization bar now renders (ComputeContextUtilization resolves a non-zero window from the threshold override). Cost is not asserted — vibe's prices are user-configurable so the adapter emits no authoritative cost. Driver: wait_turn is a stubbed SEAM; record ports it (driver-gap) before recording. BACKFLOW (live recording): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready before the real session_<id> transcript dir appears, then reconciles to a DIFFERENT session_id, so the birth is modeled as two phases (presession_birth → session_birth new_session) templated from the codex sibling, with every post-birth phase anchored same_session_as session_birth. OBSERVATIONS reduced to agent-only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false): the offline replay golden stages only transcript.jsonl, so vibe's meta.json sidecar model + tokens are unverifiable offline (same as kiro-cli) — the model + context surface is verified LIVE at record, not in the golden.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
User prompt (What is 6 times 7?) -> working on the real session

Turn completes back to ready. ModelName=mistral-medium-3.5, context_window (auto_compact_threshold) and context_utilization are verified LIVE at record via the meta.json sidecar, not in the offline golden
